title: Last-frame chaining extends video in chunks
canonical_summary: Use the final frame of one segment as the start image for the next segment.

title: Chunk length balances continuity and memory
canonical_summary: Shorter chunks are safer; longer chunks may reduce stitch points but increase VRAM pressure.
source_priority_rule: Prefer canonical lane guidance and source-backed project files over memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Explain memory/continuity tradeoff and test smaller chunks first.
exactness_guard: Do not invent exact commands, paths, class names, model names, or source claims without retrieved evidence.

title: Continuity needs more than one setting
canonical_summary: Last-frame carryover helps but prompt consistency, motion settings, and reference conditioning may also matter.
source_priority_rule: Prefer canonical lane guidance and source-backed project files over memory or old notes.

title: Next chunk resets the scene
canonical_summary: Scene reset may mean the next run was not properly anchored to the previous final frame.
source_priority_rule: Prefer canonical lane guidance and source-backed project files over memory or old notes.

title: Need chaining vs need interpolation
canonical_summary: Chaining generates new continuation; interpolation smooths or increases frames between existing frames.
source_priority_rule: Prefer canonical lane guidance and source-backed project files over memory or old notes.

title: Do not confuse chaining with one long generation
user_phrasing: why not just generate all frames at once
canonical_summary: Chaining breaks long video into manageable segments; one long generation attempts the full clip at once.
source_priority_rule: Prefer canonical lane guidance and source-backed project files over memory or old notes.
answer_pattern: Explain chaining as a chunking strategy with continuity tradeoffs.

title: How to answer last-frame chaining questions
